div1.entryFree: Dam, vb., to stop up: the strait pass was --ed with dead men, Cymb. V, 3, 11. Joined with up: voice --ed up with woe, Lucr. 1661. the more thou --est it (the fire) up, the more it burns, Gentl. II, 7, 24 (cf. the following simile). I'll have the current --ed up, H4A III, 1, 101. now will I d. up this thy yawning mouth, H6B IV, 1, 73.
